payload.summary_for_chart: Mr. Smith has multiple concerning symptoms over the past 2 years, including unintentional weight loss, persistent epigastric discomfort, nausea, bloating, early satiety, pruritus, and intermittent back pain. His A1c has increased on multiple occasions, suggesting worsening diabetes control. Mildly abnormal LFTs were noted in 2019. Multiple abdominal imaging studies (ultrasound, CT) have been requested but their completion status is unclear from the chart. Given the persistence and constellation of symptoms, particularly the unintentional weight loss and new/worsening diabetes, further investigation is warranted to rule out an underlying malignancy like pancreatic cancer. Follow-up is crucial to clarify imaging status and assess for progression.
suggested_orders: Review and clarify status of previously requested abdominal imaging (ultrasound, CT)
Repeat CBC and LFTs
Consider CA 19-9 if imaging is unrevealing or concerning
suggested_referrals: Referral to Gastroenterology for further evaluation and potential endoscopy
Consider referral to Oncology if imaging or further workup is highly suspicious
safety_netting: Instruct patient on red-flag symptoms including worsening jaundice, severe abdominal pain, or significant changes in bowel habits.
Ensure clear documentation of imaging completion and results.
Schedule follow-up appointment to review investigation results and clinical status.

payload.reason_for_referral: Referral requested due to persistent concerning symptoms including unintentional weight loss, worsening diabetes (A1c increase), epigastric discomfort, nausea, bloating, early satiety, pruritus, and intermittent back pain documented over multiple visits. Mildly abnormal LFTs were noted in 2019. The constellation of symptoms, particularly the weight loss and new/worsening diabetes, raises concern for an underlying malignancy. Further investigation is warranted.
attach_documents: List of requested imaging (ultrasound, CT) with dates
admin_notes: Clarify status of requested abdominal imaging (ultrasound, CT) completion and results.
Confirm patient contact information.
Ensure referral is directed appropriately based on initial assessment (Gastroenterology vs. Oncology).

payload.letter_body:
Clinic Name
DATE: 2023-10-27

TO: Gastroenterology/Oncology
FAX: [Insert Fax Number]

RE: Referral for Taylor Smith, DOB: 1982-02-01, OHIP: 3286-854-204

Patient Information:
Name: Taylor Smith
DOB: 1982-02-01
OHIP No: 3286-854-204
Address: 702 Leslie St, Richmond Hill, ON
Phone: 905-758-2424

Dear Colleague,

Reason for Referral:
I am referring Mr. Taylor Smith for evaluation of persistent concerning symptoms, including unintentional weight loss, worsening diabetes control, and abdominal discomfort, raising concern for an underlying malignancy, potentially pancreatic cancer.

Background:
Mr. Smith is a 44-year-old male with a history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes. He has presented multiple times over the past two years with a constellation of symptoms.

Key Clinical Findings:
Symptoms include unintentional weight loss (documented 2020-05-19, 2021-01-14, 2021-05-26), persistent epigastric discomfort, nausea, bloating, early satiety, pruritus (documented 2019-09-08, 2020-08-25), and intermittent back pain (documented 2019-02-08, 2020-01-05, 2021-05-26). His A1c has increased on multiple occasions, suggesting worsening diabetes control. Mildly abnormal LFTs were noted in 2019.

Investigations Completed:
CBC and LFTs reviewed on multiple occasions; no critical abnormalities documented in recent reviews (2020-01-05, 2021-05-26). Abdominal ultrasound and CT abdomen have been requested on multiple occasions (2019-05-15, 2020-01-05, 2020-08-25, 2021-05-26), but the completion status and results are unclear from the chart.

Relevant History & Medications:
PMH: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes.
Medications: Not available in chart.

Request:
Given the persistence and constellation of symptoms, particularly the unintentional weight loss and new/worsening diabetes, further investigation is warranted. I would appreciate your expert evaluation and management.

Sincerely,
[Your Name/Clinic Name]
attachments: List of requested imaging (ultrasound, CT) with dates

instructions: Follow up with your doctor to discuss your symptoms and review any test results.
If you have been referred to a specialist, attend your appointment.
If you have been asked to complete imaging tests (like ultrasound or CT scan), please do so as soon as possible.
Contact your doctor if you develop new or worsening symptoms, such as yellowing of the skin or eyes (jaundice), severe abdominal pain, or significant changes in bowel habits.
Continue managing your diabetes and hypertension as directed by your doctor.
Keep track of your symptoms, including weight changes, pain, and any digestive issues.
Attend your scheduled follow-up appointments to monitor your health.

payload.summary_for_chart: This 65-year-old female patient presents with a constellation of concerning symptoms including persistent dyspepsia, intermittent back pain, unintentional weight loss, new-onset diabetes, early satiety, dark urine, bloating, and decreased appetite documented across multiple visits since 2019. Mildly abnormal LFTs have been noted repeatedly, while lipase has been normal on recent checks. Notably, there is no recent imaging available in the chart, and prior imaging (CT abdomen, MRI) discussed in 2019-2020 was either not completed or status is unclear. Given the persistence of multiple alarm symptoms and lack of recent imaging, further investigation is warranted to evaluate for underlying pancreatic pathology.
suggested_orders: Review results of prior imaging (CT/MRI) if available
Order abdominal imaging (CT or MRI) if not recently performed
Repeat CMP including LFTs
suggested_referrals: Referral to Gastroenterology or Oncology for further evaluation
safety_netting: Monitor for worsening symptoms, including jaundice, severe abdominal pain, or significant weight loss.
Instruct patient on red-flag symptoms requiring urgent attention.
Ensure follow-up on pending investigations and imaging results.

payload.reason_for_referral: Referral requested for persistent concerning symptoms including dyspepsia, back pain, unintentional weight loss, new-onset diabetes, early satiety, dark urine, bloating, and decreased appetite documented across multiple visits since 2019. Mildly abnormal LFTs are noted repeatedly. Lack of recent imaging is a concern, with prior imaging status unclear. Further investigation is warranted to evaluate for underlying pancreatic pathology.
admin_notes: Confirm availability of prior imaging results (CT/MRI)
Verify patient contact information

payload.letter_body:
Clinic Name
DATE: 2023-10-27

TO: Gastroenterology or Oncology
FAX: [FAX NUMBER]

RE: Jordan Garcia, DOB: 1960-02-28, OHIP: 5554-564-750

Patient Information:
Name: Jordan Garcia
DOB: 1960-02-28
OHIP: 5554-564-750
Address: 864 Yonge St, Richmond Hill, ON
Phone: 905-366-7065

Dear Colleague,

Reason for Referral:
Referral for evaluation of persistent concerning symptoms suggestive of potential pancreatic pathology.

Background:
Ms. Garcia is a 65-year-old female with a history of hyperlipidemia and hypertension. She has presented with multiple concerning symptoms since 2019, including persistent dyspepsia, intermittent back pain, unintentional weight loss, new-onset diabetes, early satiety, dark urine, bloating, and decreased appetite. Mildly abnormal LFTs have been noted repeatedly.

Key Clinical Findings:
- Persistent dyspepsia (documented across multiple visits since 2019)
- Intermittent back pain (documented across multiple visits since 2019)
- Unintentional weight loss (documented 2020-05-11)
- New-onset diabetes (documented 2020-12-21)
- Early satiety (documented 2019-05-02, 2021-05-18)
- Dark urine (documented 2019-05-02, 2019-09-13, 2020-08-27)
- Decreased appetite (documented 2021-05-18)
- Bloating (documented 2020-08-27)
- Mildly abnormal LFTs (repeatedly noted)
- Lipase within normal range (documented 2020-08-27, 2021-05-18)
- No critical CBC abnormalities (documented 2020-01-07)

Investigations Completed:
- CBC (2020-01-07): No critical abnormalities.
- CMP/LFTs (multiple dates): Mildly abnormal.
- Lipase (2020-08-27, 2021-05-18): Within normal range.

Relevant History & Medications:
- PMH: Hyperlipidemia, Hypertension.
- Medications: Not available in chart.
- Imaging: No recent abdominal imaging available in chart. CT abdomen discussed in 2019-09-13 (completion status unclear). MRI deferred pending specialist advice on 2019-05-02 and 2020-08-27.

Request:
Given the constellation of persistent alarm symptoms and lack of recent imaging, I request your expert evaluation to investigate for underlying pancreatic pathology, including pancreatic cancer. Please review the attached documents and consider appropriate further investigations and management.

Sincerely,

[Your Name/Clinic Name]

instructions: Follow up with your specialist as recommended.
Keep track of your symptoms, such as changes in appetite, weight, pain, or urine color.
Contact your doctor if you develop jaundice (yellowing of skin or eyes).
Contact your doctor if you experience severe abdominal pain.
Contact your doctor if you have significant unintentional weight loss.
Attend all scheduled appointments and complete any recommended tests or imaging.
Discuss any new or worsening symptoms with your healthcare provider.
Ensure your specialist receives any requested prior imaging results.

payload.summary_for_chart: Mr. Wong is a 69-year-old male with persistent dyspepsia, dark urine, decreased appetite, and new-onset diabetes since 2019. Multiple abdominal ultrasounds have been requested across visits but completion status is unclear. A CT abdomen was discussed in December 2020, but its completion is also uncertain. Lipase has been within normal range, but ferritin results remain pending from July and December 2020. Given the constellation of symptoms and incomplete investigations, follow-up is warranted to clarify the status of imaging and labs and reassess symptoms.
suggested_orders: Confirm completion status of previously requested abdominal ultrasound
Confirm completion status of CT abdomen discussed in Dec 2020
Review pending ferritin results from July and Dec 2020
Repeat CMP if not recently done
suggested_referrals: Consider GI referral if imaging and labs are unrevealing or symptoms persist/worsen
safety_netting: Document any new symptoms, especially jaundice, significant weight loss, or worsening abdominal pain.
Advise patient to return if symptoms worsen or new concerning symptoms develop.
Ensure follow-up to review imaging and lab results.

payload.reason_for_referral: Referral requested for persistent dyspepsia, dark urine, decreased appetite, and new-onset diabetes in a 69-year-old male. Multiple abdominal ultrasounds have been requested across visits, and a CT abdomen was discussed in December 2020, but completion status is unclear. Pending ferritin results from July and December 2020 also remain outstanding, contributing to the semi-urgent nature due to incomplete investigation status.
admin_notes: Confirm completion status of abdominal ultrasound and CT abdomen.

Ensure referral contact details are accurate.

payload.letter_body:
Dr. [Recipient Name]

DATE: 2024-07-26

Clinic Name

Address: 561 Leslie St, Richmond Hill, ON
Phone: 905-936-4228

Dear Colleague,

Reason for Referral:
I am referring Mr. Alex Garcia, a 59-year-old male, for evaluation concerning potential pancreatic cancer, given a constellation of persistent symptoms and risk factors.

Background:
Mr. Garcia has presented with multiple concerning symptoms over the past several years, including new-onset diabetes (diagnosed 2020-04-03), progressive weight loss indicators (decreased appetite noted 2019-11-26, early satiety noted 2019-07-10, 2020-07-08, 2021-03-21), persistent pruritus (noted 2019-07-10, 2020-07-08, 2021-03-21, 2021-07-21), and epigastric discomfort/dyspepsia (noted 2019-07-10, 2020-11-29). Intermittent back pain was noted in 2020-04-03, and his A1c has been increasing (noted 2020-07-08, 2020-11-29).

Key Clinical Findings:
- New-onset diabetes mellitus (2020-04-03)
- Progressive weight loss indicators: decreased appetite, early satiety
- Persistent pruritus
- Epigastric discomfort/dyspepsia
- Intermittent back pain
- Increasing A1c

Investigations Completed:
- Lipase was within normal range on 2020-04-03.
- CMPs have been monitored, with increasing A1c noted.
- Status of previously discussed CT abdomen (2020-11-29, 2021-07-21) is unclear.

Relevant History & Medications:

- Medications: List of medications attached.

Request:
Given the concerning symptoms and unclear imaging status, I would appreciate your expert evaluation of Mr. Garcia to investigate the possibility of pancreatic cancer and determine the need for further workup or management.

Sincerely,

[Your Name/Clinic Name]

instructions: Please follow up with your doctor to discuss the status of your abdominal imaging (CT scan discussed in late 2020 and mid-2021).
If you haven't had recent imaging, your doctor may order a new scan.
Keep track of any new or worsening symptoms, such as jaundice (yellowing of skin/eyes), severe abdominal pain, or significant weight loss.
Contact your doctor if you experience any concerning new symptoms or if your current symptoms get worse.
Continue to monitor your blood sugar as advised by your doctor, especially with your new-onset diabetes.
Attend all scheduled follow-up appointments to discuss your symptoms and any test results.
If you have questions or concerns about your health, please reach out to your healthcare team.
